contents.value: **MODULE** — Modula-3 keyword.
**INTERFACE** — Modula-3 keyword.
**IMPORT** — Modula-3 keyword.
**FROM** — Modula-3 keyword.
**EXPORT** — Modula-3 keyword.
**NEW** — Built-in function/command in Modula-3.
**INC** — Built-in function/command in Modula-3.
**DEC** — Built-in function/command in Modula-3.
**ABS** — Built-in function/command in Modula-3.
**FLOAT** — Built-in function/command in Modula-3.
**let** binds a value to a name (immutable by default).
**match** performs pattern matching against a value.
**type** defines a new type alias or algebraic data type.
A **record** groups named fields into a product type.
A **variant** (sum type) represents one of several possible shapes.
A **function** maps input values to output values. First-class in functional languages.
**Recursion** occurs when a function calls itself to solve smaller subproblems.
**Pattern matching** destructures values and dispatches based on shape.
A **tuple** is a fixed-size product of heterogeneous values.
**Immutable** values cannot be changed after creation.
A **higher-order function** takes functions as arguments or returns them.
**Currying** transforms a multi-argument function into a sequence of single-argument functions.
A **monad** is an abstraction for sequencing computations with context (e.g., Option, Result).
**fold** (reduce) combines elements of a collection into a single result.
**map** applies a function to each element of a collection, producing a new collection.

```m3
TEXT
```
The built-in immutable string type in Modula-3, representing a sequence of characters with reference semantics. TEXT values are garbage-collected and support concatenation with the ampersand operator.

```m3
INTEGER
```
The built-in signed integer type in Modula-3, representing whole numbers within the platform-dependent range. Supports standard arithmetic, comparison, and bitwise operations defined by the language.

```m3
REAL
```
The standard single-precision floating-point type in Modula-3, conforming to IEEE 754 representation. Used for numeric computations requiring fractional values with moderate precision.

```m3
LONGREAL
```
The double-precision floating-point type in Modula-3, providing higher precision than REAL for scientific and mathematical computations requiring greater accuracy in decimal representation.

```m3
BOOLEAN = {FALSE, TRUE}
```
The built-in enumeration type with exactly two values, FALSE and TRUE, used for logical operations, conditionals, and control flow in Modula-3 programs.

```m3
CHAR
```
The built-in character type representing a single 8-bit character in the ISO Latin-1 character set. CHAR values are ordinal and support ORD and VAL operations for conversion.

```m3
ARRAY [index_type] OF element_type
```
A fixed-size or open array type in Modula-3 that stores elements of a uniform type indexed by an ordinal type. Open arrays use ARRAY OF T syntax and are passed by reference.

```m3
RECORD field1: Type1; field2: Type2; END
```
A composite value type in Modula-3 that groups named fields of potentially different types into a single structured data unit, similar to structs in C or records in Pascal.

```m3
THREAD
```
The standard Modula-3 interface for concurrent programming, providing thread creation via Fork, synchronization with Mutex and Condition variables, and cooperative scheduling primitives.

```m3
Fmt
```
The standard formatting interface in Modula-3 providing functions to convert integers, reals, booleans, and characters to their TEXT representations for output and string construction.

```m3
Wr
```
The standard writer interface in Modula-3 providing buffered output streams for writing text to files, standard output, and other destinations with flush and close operations.

```m3
Rd
```
The standard reader interface in Modula-3 providing buffered input streams for reading characters and lines from files, standard input, and other sources with EOF detection.

```m3
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```m3
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```m3
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```m3
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```m3
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```m3
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```m3
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```m3
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```m3
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
